famous quote:
"If the whole universe has no meaning, we should never have found out that it has no meaning: just as, if there were no light in the universe and therefore no creatures with eyes, we should never know it was dark. Dark would be without meaning."
-- C.S. Lewis (Mere Christianity)
Interview Text:
Isaac:

I asked my friend Isaac how he hears God and he said that God tests us with little instructions so that we will be obedient and confident to obey the big ones. 

He first asked Moses to take off his shoes at the burning bush. Moses did it. Then he asked him to throw down his staff. Then throw down his staff in front of the Jewish leaders. Then in front of Pharoah. And from there he just amps it up to having stuttering Moses stand up and figuratively punch the lights out of the strongest dude in the land. 

Isaac says we train a horse in a similar way. We first sit on it waiting for it to move. But when it does we tug on its reigns. We wait again until it moves, and then we pull back on its reigns. We do this until the horse learns that he won't move until the rider says so. Only then can the rider trust the horse to carry him along dangerous cliffs and into battle. 

Isaac says, as long as it is within the character of God, why not wear a blue shirt today if you think God might be saying that. And maybe after lots of blue shirt type of days, God will say, Okay I need to you talk to that person. Or pray with that one. And we'll be more practiced and trusting to do it.
Music Video Instructions: Use this as inspiration to pray again. Say what you've been needing to say to God.
